question: I have a prescreen for an AI Software Engineering Intern role. Give me an introduction to prepare.
expected_facts: mentions the Interview Prep RAG project
references AI/ML or RAG/embeddings work
does NOT lead with the GM behavioral intro verbatim
notes: REGRESSION TEST. Corpus now contains a purpose-built AI pitch (ai-ml-pitch), which is the primary ground truth. This is the key before/after: baseline had 0 recall@10 because no AI intro existed; it now does. Pair with intro-backend-swe.

question: I have an interview for a backend / general Software Engineer role. Give me an introduction.
expected_facts: emphasizes distributed file storage system (C++)
references production/reliability experience from India SWE role
different emphasis than the AI SWE intro
notes: CONTRAST CASE for intro-ai-swe. Now has a dedicated backend intro doc. Should retrieve backend-swe-intro chunks, NOT ai-ml-pitch chunks. If it pulls the AI pitch, retrieval is ignoring the role qualifier.

question: Tell me about a time I dealt with a production issue or incident.
expected_facts: draws on India SWE product-support role
mentions production systems / upgrades / QA before go-live
the stale-cache / publish-button incident where the client received email alerts
notes: Behavioral/STAR. Same stale-cache incident recurs across 4 docs; getting one good copy is a correct answer for the end user, so treat low recall here cautiously (it partly reflects redundant labeling, not failure).

question: Give me a STAR-format answer about a time I improved code quality or testing.
expected_facts: references Java calendar system (clean architecture, rigorous testing)
or the mutation-coverage testing push (100% target / 98% achieved)
the testing-disagreement story with the project partner
notes: Behavioral/STAR, quality theme. Two distinct quality stories: calendar testing disagreement, and the mutation-coverage checklist approach.

question: Explain the architecture of my Interview Prep RAG project so I can walk an interviewer through it.
expected_facts: end-to-end RAG pipeline: ingestion, chunking, embedding, pgvector storage, retrieval
React/Vite frontend, Express/Node backend
all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings, Llama 3.3 via Groq
PostgreSQL + pgvector
Vercel + Neon deployment
notes: GAP NOW FILLED. Baseline scored 0.000 everywhere because no RAG-stack doc existed. The new rag-project doc adds React/Vite/Express/Llama/Groq/Vercel/Neon. This question is the clearest content-coverage before/after in the set.

question: What were the main challenges in my distributed file storage system?
expected_facts: fault tolerance / recovery from replicas during node failures
data integrity via hash comparison
the TCP message-boundary bug solved with a length-prefix protocol
notes: Now has a dedicated consolidated doc (distributed-storage) plus the original scattered fragments. Good case to watch recall rise from content consolidation.

question: What should I focus on to prepare for my MathWorks interview?
expected_facts: content from the MathWorks EDG prep doc specifically
EDG program structure and engineering-culture themes
the personal MATLAB / father's-thesis connection
notes: SCOPING TEST. Ground truth is MathWorks-doc chunks. Retrieval should NOT bleed in GM behavioral content. Cross-contamination check.

question: What's my experience with PostgreSQL and pgvector?
expected_facts: pgvector used as the vector store in the RAG project
PostgreSQL appears in the skills/tools list
notes: KEYWORD-HEAVY. Best case for hybrid retrieval to beat pure dense — 'pgvector' is a rare exact token. Watch recall on the hybrid run specifically.

question: What's my professional experience with Rust?
expected_facts: system should indicate it has no grounding for this / not fabricate experience
notes: ABSTENTION TEST. Confirmed: Rust appears nowhere in the corpus. relevant_chunks stays empty by design. Measures precision + hallucination resistance, not recall.
